title: 6th-Grade Antique Storage Trunk
description: A distressed wooden dome-top steamer trunk in Reagan's workspace that replaces standard plastic bins to store physical notebooks, sketchbooks, and art pieces.
instructions: Set up the trunk as her permanent home base. Sort notebooks, sketchbooks, and finished art into the trunk so everything physical lives in one beautiful place instead of plastic bins.

title: Epoch-Specific History Boxes
description: Hands-on curriculum kits (like History Unboxed) containing replica artifacts, historical maps, and era-specific crafts for the period she is studying.
instructions: Open one era box at a time. Handle the replica artifacts, lay out the map, and complete the era craft. Reagan logs what each object tells us about daily life in that period.

title: "Gold Items" & Period Replicas
description: Faux-ancient coins, stamped metal seals, and vintage-style jewelry matching the specific historical timeline she explores.
instructions: Match each replica to its era on the timeline. Reagan describes what the object was used for (trade, status, sealing letters) and adds it to her history display.

title: Biographical Artifact Boxes
description: A handmade shoebox "museum" display featuring 5 physical objects or illustrations representing major turning points from a biography.
instructions: After reading a biography, Reagan chooses 5 turning points and represents each with an object or drawing inside the shoebox museum. She writes a one-line label for each like a real exhibit.

title: Time-Capsule Curation
description: A small physical box filled with handwritten citizen journals, hand-drawn maps, and physical artifacts from a specific era.
instructions: Reagan writes a journal entry as a citizen from the era, draws a map of their town, and adds a couple of 'artifacts'. Seal it as a time capsule that tells the era's story.

title: Cryptographic Symbol Decoder Wheel
description: A physical, dual-ring rotating cipher wheel made out of chipboard to decode vocabulary, historical scripts, and secret assignment clues.
instructions: Build a two-ring cipher wheel (outer A-Z, inner shifted A-Z). Use it to decode the day's secret clue and vocabulary. See the printable Idea Book for three ready-made cipher clues.

title: Blueprint Scaling Projects
description: Mapping out a physical room or yard plot on real grid paper using a tape measure to calculate perimeter and surface area without digital coordinates.
instructions: Measure a real room or yard plot. Pick a scale (e.g., 1 square = 1 foot), draw it to scale on grid paper, then calculate perimeter and area from the drawing.

title: Isometric Grid Blueprinting
description: Translating 3D Minecraft or Roblox structures onto physical isometric paper, calculating volume by counting block scales.
instructions: Reagan recreates one of her favorite game builds on isometric paper, then counts the blocks to compute total volume and surface area. Ties her game love straight into geometry.

title: Geometric Friendship Patterns
description: Crafting custom bracelets using mathematical row counts to discover algebraic symmetry and repeating sequences completely offline.
instructions: Reagan designs a repeating bracelet pattern, writes out the row counts as a number sequence, and finds the symmetry/repeat rule before knotting it.

title: Mineral Hardness Test Lab
description: Using a physical scratch-test kit (real copper penny, unglazed tile, steel nail, glass) to log properties on a Mohs scale sheet.
instructions: Scratch each sample with the testers in order of hardness and record where it falls on the Mohs scale. Reagan ranks her rocks from softest to hardest.

title: Cosmetic Emulsion Lab
description: Mixing precise volume ratios of natural oils, beeswax, and safe botanical extracts to calculate melting points and volume ratios for custom skin balms.
instructions: With an adult handling heat, measure oils and beeswax in exact ratios, melt and blend, then pour into tins. Reagan records the ratio and observes the solid↔liquid melting point.

title: Effervescent Bath Chemistry
description: Balancing acid-base ratios by mixing dry baking soda and citric acid to observe real gas-release reactions (bath bombs / fizzers).
instructions: Measure baking soda and citric acid in a balanced ratio, mist lightly, press into molds. Drop one in water to watch the acid-base gas reaction fizz.

title: Solar Print Botany Kit (Cyanotype)
description: Using light-sensitive cyanotype paper outdoors to create beautiful physical sun-print silhouettes of leaves, flower petals, and ferns.
instructions: Arrange botanical specimens on cyanotype paper, expose in direct sun, then rinse in water to reveal a deep-blue sun print. Reagan labels each plant she collected.

title: Physical Circuit Engineering
description: Using offline, snap-together electronics boards to build real, functioning motors, lights, and audible alarms.
instructions: Follow the kit guide to build a working circuit (light, motor, or alarm). Reagan sketches the circuit path and explains how the electricity flows in a loop.

title: Stratified Swamp Basin Replica
description: Building a highly detailed miniature wetland diorama in a shallow watertight basin using real peat, Spanish moss, twigs, and resin layers.
instructions: Layer the basin to show wetland strata (water, mud, plants). Reagan labels each layer and the creatures that would live there, building a real ecosystem model.

title: Ornithology Field Journals
description: A physical leather-bound nature sketchbook paired with illustrated field guides to log local bird behaviors and calls.
instructions: Sit quietly outdoors, sketch and note each bird's color, size, and call. Reagan looks it up in the field guide and records the date, weather, and behavior.

title: Backyard Quadrat Studies
description: Placing a physical 1x1 meter PVC square in outdoor spaces to count, classify, and sketch local plant and insect biodiversity.
instructions: Drop the quadrat in a patch of yard. Count and classify every plant and insect inside it, then sketch the most interesting find. Compare two different spots.

title: Pencil-and-Paper Aerodynamics
description: Building different styles of paper planes to test drag, lift, and wind resistance using a physical tape measure to graph flight distances.
instructions: Fold 3 plane designs, fly each 3 times, measure distances, and graph the results. Reagan decides which design flew best and why (lift vs. drag).

title: DIY Nesting Box Project
description: Building simple bird nesting boxes or backyard insect hotels to blend design, tool safety, and environmental science.
instructions: With adult supervision, build a nesting box or insect hotel from a plan. Mount it outside and Reagan logs any visitors over the following weeks.

title: Wetland Ecosystem Field Mapping
description: Visiting local natural wetlands, preserves, or parks to log living micro-habitats, map mud topography, and sample wild flora.
instructions: Visit a local wetland or preserve. Reagan maps the habitats she sees, notes the wildlife, and brings back sketches and notes for her field journal.

title: Textile & Weaving History Study
description: Touring living history landmarks or regional textile exhibits to sketch antique spinning equipment and document historical clothing methods.
instructions: Tour a living-history site or textile exhibit. Reagan sketches the spinning/weaving tools and writes how clothing was made before machines.

title: Geological Landmark Survey
description: Exploring regional public nature preserves or caves to map physical rock layers, find quartz variations, and document active erosion patterns.
instructions: Visit a preserve, gorge, or cave. Reagan photographs and sketches the rock layers, looks for quartz, and notes where water is shaping the land (erosion).

title: Local Living History Excursion
description: Field trips to regional landmarks, physical libraries, historical markers, and local archives instead of online reading modules.
instructions: Visit a landmark, library, or archive. Reagan reads the historical markers, talks to a librarian/docent, and brings home one primary-source story to retell.

title: Scale-Model Blueprint Embroidery
description: Translating custom room designs or geometry dimensions onto muslin fabric, using a basic backstitch to outline perimeter bounds.
instructions: Reagan draws a simple room/shape blueprint on muslin, then backstitches the outline. A calming, sensory way to practice measuring and perimeter.

title: Botanical Dye Cross-Stitching
description: Hand-stitching simple biological patterns or bird vectors onto high-gauge aida cloth using threads tinted from backyard botanical extracts.
instructions: Optionally tint thread with plant dye, then cross-stitch a simple bird or plant pattern. Quiet fine-motor practice that doubles as color science.

title: Upcycled Canvas Tool Case
description: Sewing a custom physical pouch from heavy scrap cloth or denim to organize field magnifying glasses, rulers, and specimen tweezers.
instructions: Reagan designs and sews a roll-up pouch for her field tools. She measures pockets to fit each tool, blending design and practical sewing.

title: Desk Feng Shui Restructuring
description: Using a tape measure and a physical inventory sheet to reorganize and optimize her antique trunk and workspace layout.
instructions: Reagan measures her space, inventories what she owns, and re-designs the layout for flow and calm. A reset block that builds planning + measurement skills.

title: Choreography Notation Graphs
description: Creating a physical floor-plan grid with symbols to map out dance movements, counts, and spatial transitions on paper.
instructions: Reagan invents symbols for dance moves and plots them on a floor-grid with counts, then performs the routine she 'coded'. Movement meets coordinate geometry.

title: 6th-Grade Metric Scavenger Hunt
description: Hidden printable checkpoints around the backyard or house using math word-problem riddles that unlock small physical items.
instructions: Hide riddle clues that each require a measurement or calculation to find the next spot. The final clue unlocks a small reward. See the Idea Book for three ready-made riddles.

title: Passive Live Nature Feeds
description: A secondary monitor locked onto a live wilderness stream (deep-sea cams, watering holes, bird cams) as a calming background during heavy focus sessions.
materials: Live cam link (zoo, aquarium, bird feeder)
instructions: Play a calming live nature cam as background during focus or as a regulation break. Optional: Reagan notes any animal she spots in her field journal.

title: Stop-Motion Brick Game Dev
description: Writing short scripts on paper and filming physical brick characters frame-by-frame on her tablet to study sequencing and plot design.
instructions: Reagan writes a short script (beginning-middle-end), then films her brick scene frame by frame. A reward block that secretly teaches story structure and sequencing.

title: The Plot Arc Matrix
description: Tracking a favorite TV show's character growth and episodic pacing on a large physical timeline poster with multi-colored sticky notes.
instructions: Reagan maps her favorite show's episodes on a timeline, using colored stickies for each character's ups and downs. Reward time that builds literary analysis.

title: Acoustic Sound Engineering
description: Experimenting with cardboard tubes, fabrics, or copper to see how materials alter sound output from her earbuds.
instructions: Reagan funnels and muffles sound with different materials to hear how each changes volume and tone, then explains which material was loudest and why.
